channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel launches airstrikes in Beirut, displacing thousands of people
item.description: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.content:encoded: Israel has carried out air strikes in central Beirut for the first time since the latest conflict began, displacing hundreds of thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: Hadeel Al-Shalchi

item.title: CNN correspondent shares his takeaways from his recent visit to Iran
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks CNN's Fred Pleitgen for his takeaways from his recent reporting trip to Iran.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Muslim voters react to Rep. Andy Ogles' comments that they 'don't belong'
item.description: Muslims in Rep. Andy Ogles', R-Tenn., district react to his characterization of them and their religion after he said they "don't belong in American society."
item.content:encoded: Muslims in Rep. Andy Ogles', R-Tenn., district react to his characterization of them and their religion after he said they "don't belong in American society."
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Bucking stigma, more places turn to factory-built for affordable housing
item.description: Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.
item.content:encoded:
Mobile homes have long been zoned out of cities and suburbs. But with updated designs and a housing shortage, they're increasingly being welcomed as more-affordable starter homes.

(Image credit: Anusha Mathur)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Medicaid can share data with ICE. Here's how that 180-degree change spreads fear
item.description: When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear getting the health coverage.
item.content:encoded:
When Medicaid began sharing personal data with federal immigration authorities last year, it upended decades of explicit promises to patients. Now, even eligible immigrants fear getting the health coverage.

(Image credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: Alex Olgin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel launches strikes in Beirut, FBI investigating two unrelated attacks in Michigan and Virginia, Senate passes bipartisan housing bill to ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.
item.content:encoded: Israel launches strikes in Beirut, FBI investigating two unrelated attacks in Michigan and Virginia, Senate passes bipartisan housing bill to ban large investors from buying up single-family homes.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: As partial shutdown drags on, Morning Edition checks out TSA lines at 3 airports
item.description: Transportation Security Administration officers have worked without pay since Feb. 14 due to the partial government shutdown. Morning Edition visited three airports to experience the security scene.
item.content:encoded: Transportation Security Administration officers have worked without pay since Feb. 14 due to the partial government shutdown. Morning Edition visited three airports to experience the security scene.
item.dc:creator: Milton Guevara

item.title: War in Iran increases demand for U.S. liquefied natural gas producers
item.description: Liquefied Natural Gas producers in the U.S. are benefiting from export demands fueled by the Iran war.
item.content:encoded: Liquefied Natural Gas producers in the U.S. are benefiting from export demands fueled by the Iran war.
item.dc:creator: Drew Hawkins

item.title: Exiled Iranians watch the war from afar, wondering what awaits when it ends
item.description: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.
item.content:encoded: Iranians who fled the country before the war with the U.S. and Israel are now watching it unfold, wondering what will happen when it ends.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: NBA teams are 'tanking' to get better draft picks. Here are some possible solutions
item.description: This NBA season has featured an epidemic of "tanking" -- teams intentionally losing games to try to secure a higher pick in next year's draft. Planet Money considers possible solutions.
item.content:encoded: This NBA season has featured an epidemic of "tanking" -- teams intentionally losing games to try to secure a higher pick in next year's draft. Planet Money considers possible solutions.
item.dc:creator: Keith Romer
